card.intuition: The 'D' in SOLID. High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions (interfaces).
card.analogy: A wall socket: your Lamp (High-level) doesn't know about the 'Electrical Grid' (Low-level). Both depend on the 'Plug' (Abstraction). If the grid changes from Coal to Solar, your lamp still works perfectly because it only cares about the 'Plug' interface.
when_to_use: Writing 'Pluggable' code where you can swap a real DB for a Mock DB in tests
Decoupling your application logic from a specific UI framework or Library
Designing systems that need to support multiple third-party providers
gotchas: It requires extra work! You have to create 'Interfaces' for everything, which can feel like YAGNI violation if you know you'll only ever use one DB.
It makes code harder to navigate initially ('Go to definition' often takes you to an interface instead of the real code).

exercise.prompt: Instead of high-level code calling low-level code directly, both should depend on a(n) ___________.
